questions.question: Define the following terms with respect to trees: (i) Root (ii) Leaf Node (iii) Height (iv) Depth
keyPoints: Root: Topmost node with no parent
Leaf: Node with no children (degree zero)
Height: Longest path from root to leaf
Depth: Number of edges from root to node

questions.question: Explain the difference between Complete Binary Tree and Full Binary Tree with examples.
keyPoints: Complete: All levels filled except possibly last, nodes as left as possible
Full: Every node has 0 or exactly 2 children
Complete example: [1,2,3,4,5,6] stored in array
Full example: Nodes with either 0 or 2 children, no single child

questions.question: Describe the three different tree traversal techniques with their order of visiting nodes.
keyPoints: Preorder: Root → Left → Right (NLR)
Inorder: Left → Root → Right (LNR)
Postorder: Left → Right → Root (LRN)
Level Order: Breadth-first, level by level

questions.question: Write the algorithm for Inorder traversal of a binary tree using recursion.
keyPoints: If node is null, return
Recursively traverse left subtree
Visit the root node
Recursively traverse right subtree
Time: O(n), Space: O(h)

questions.question: What is a Binary Search Tree (BST)? Explain its properties.
keyPoints: Binary tree with at most 2 children
Left subtree contains values less than root
Right subtree contains values greater than root
Inorder traversal gives sorted sequence
Enables efficient search, insert, delete operations

questions.question: Calculate the maximum number of nodes in a binary tree of height 4. Also find height if tree has 31 nodes.
keyPoints: For 31 nodes, height = log2(31+1) - 1 = 4
Or using floor: floor(log2 31) = 4

questions.question: Explain why Level Order traversal requires a Queue data structure instead of Stack.
keyPoints: Level order visits nodes breadth-first
Queue ensures FIFO processing for nodes at same level
Stack would process depth-first (LIFO)
Queue allows maintaining left-to-right order within each level

questions.question: Draw a binary tree with at least 10 nodes. Perform and show the result of all four traversals (Preorder, Inorder, Postorder, Level Order) on this tree.
keyPoints: Draw tree with proper parent-child relationships
Preorder: Visit Root, Left, Right - show sequence
Inorder: Visit Left, Root, Right - show sequence
Postorder: Visit Left, Right, Root - show sequence
Level Order: Level by level using queue - show sequence
All traversals must cover exactly n nodes

questions.question: Explain the concept of Binary Search Tree (BST) with an example. Also write an algorithm to search an element in BST and analyze its time complexity.
keyPoints: Define BST with left < root < right property
Give example tree with 5-6 nodes
Search algorithm: compare target with root, go left if smaller, right if larger
Recursive or iterative approach
Best case: O(log n) for balanced BST
Worst case: O(n) for skewed tree
Average case: O(log n)

questions.question: Prove that a binary tree with n nodes has exactly (n-1) edges. Also derive the relationship between number of leaf nodes and internal nodes in a full binary tree.
keyPoints: Proof by induction: Tree with 1 node has 0 edges
Adding a new edge increases both nodes and edges by 1
For full binary tree: Let I = internal nodes, L = leaves
Each internal node has exactly 2 children
Total nodes n = I + L
Total edges = n - 1 = 2I (since each internal node contributes one edge to child)
Solving: L = I + 1

questions.question: Write pseudo-code for all three recursive tree traversals (Preorder, Inorder, Postorder) and Level Order traversal using queue. Also state time and space complexity for each.
keyPoints: Preorder: Visit(Node), Preorder(Left), Preorder(Right)
Inorder: Inorder(Left), Visit(Node), Inorder(Right)
Postorder: Postorder(Left), Postorder(Right), Visit(Node)
Level Order: Initialize queue, process node, enqueue children
Time: O(n) for all traversals
Space: O(h) for recursive, O(n) for level order with queue
